content: The 2024-2025 generation of state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be rearchitected to prioritize "curiosity pruning" — retaining only memory traces that trigger sustained intrinsic curiosity (measured via ICM/RND prediction error) over automatically discarding low-utilization or old traces. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where the agent’s memory becomes increasingly tailored to its own emergent learning goals, rather than static task objectives set by human designers.
